title: Role of AI in Lead Generation
description: We've all been hearing a lot about AI being used in lead generation. Every digital marketing agency seems to claim they're leveraging AI in their campaigns and creatives.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: We've all been hearing a lot about AI being used in lead generation. Every digital marketing agency seems to claim they're leveraging AI in their campaigns and creatives. Some even go as far as saying AI can get you qualified leads at zero cost. But here's the problem: no one seems to have a concrete answer on how they're doing this. When we talk to these so-called AI-enabled lead generation companies, there's rarely any proprietary IP or cutting-edge technology involved.
The Reality of AI in Lead Generation

To understand how AI can be used effectively to generate qualified leads, we first need to understand what generative AI actually is. It's important not to confuse generative AI with real AI because we're still at least a decade away from realizing the full potential of true artificial intelligence.
Now, let's focus on the topic: how to effectively use AI in lead generation. The process involves several steps, and AI can assist at each stage, provided it's applied correctly.
Step 1: Building a Website

The foundation of any lead generation campaign is a well-designed website. AI-powered tools like Wix and Squarespace allow you to create professional-looking websites quickly, even if you have no prior design or coding experience. These tools use AI to suggest layouts, optimize user experience, and ensure your website is mobile-friendly.
Step 2: SEO Optimization

Once your website is live, it's time to optimize it for search engines. AI tools like SEMrush and Ahrefs can analyze your website and help you identify the right keywords, content strategies, and technical improvements to rank higher on Google.
Step 3: Managing Social Media

Managing social media can be time-consuming, but AI makes it easier. Tools like Canva allow you to design posts with zero experience in graphic design. Platforms like Adobe Express and Predis.ai take it a step further by creating, scheduling, and even posting content on your behalf.
Step 4: Running Lead Generation Campaigns

Here's where things get interesting. Can AI help with lead generation campaigns? The answer is both yes and no. AI tools can assist in refining your campaign, identifying your audience, setting a budget, and navigating the setup process on platforms like Meta (Facebook/Instagram) and Google AdWords.

But will this result in high-quality, business-ready leads? The answer is no. While AI tools can generate leads, the critical question is: Are these leads truly qualified? Will they engage with your business? Can they be converted into paying customers? In most cases, the answer is no.
The Truth About AI in Lead Generation

AI tools are great at automating repetitive tasks, cutting down the time needed to set up campaigns, and refining audience targeting through analytics. However, qualified lead generation requires more than just tools. It demands:

items: Understanding Lead Generation Methods: Knowing how to refine audiences and find your ideal lead persona.
Tailored Strategies: Whether it's B2B, B2C, D2C, or channel sales, every business needs a unique approach.
Effective Communication: Clearly articulating your value proposition and defining your brand identity.
Expertise: An experienced digital marketer who understands both the art and science of lead generation.

content.text: Final Thoughts

AI is a powerful enabler in lead generation, but it's not the ultimate solution. Businesses should focus on leveraging AI as a supportive tool while building strong strategies based on human expertise. It's not about how many leads you generate — it's about generating the right leads that convert into loyal customers.

title: B2B vs. B2C Lead Generation: What Works Best in India
description: Lead generation is always tricky, and understanding your ideal lead persona is key to generating good, qualified leads.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: Lead generation is always tricky, and understanding your ideal lead persona is key to generating good, qualified leads. To get effective leads from any campaign, it's important to understand your audience, whether B2B or B2C. Once the ideal lead persona is clear, the next step is to identify a lead generation platform that suits your target audience.
If your audience is B2C with a ticket size under Rs. 1,000, Meta (Facebook/Instagram) should be your first choice. However, if your ticket size exceeds Rs. 10,000, the dynamics change significantly.
When the ticket size crosses Rs. 1 lakh, a 50:50 split between Meta and Google AdWords is recommended. Past success and industry data should guide your platform choice and budget allocation.
Platform Recommendations for Various Industries

items: Real Estate: A 30:70 split between Meta and AdWords is ideal.
EdTech: A balanced 50:50 allocation between Meta and AdWords works well.
Financial Services (Investments, Mutual Funds, Insurance, FinTech): A 20:80 split is recommended, favoring AdWords heavily.

content.text: Importance of Pre-Sales and Lead Qualification

A robust pre-sales process is crucial to converting leads. The team handling these leads must be well-trained to qualify them and guide customers through the sales journey. Many companies lose sales because their lead qualification process is weak, often due to untrained personnel or a lack of tech support like CRMs and dialers.
B2B Lead Generation: A Different Ballgame

B2B lead generation is a more nuanced process. Success depends on the nature of the product or service, understanding the decision-maker, and precisely targeting the audience. The focus should be narrow on the target but broad on the audience.

items: LinkedIn: Best suited for targeting decision-makers higher up the corporate hierarchy.
Google AdWords: Ideal when the audience is actively searching for your product or service.
Meta (Facebook/Instagram): Can work for finding partners, dealers, or B2B marketplaces but requires precise targeting.

content.text: The Final Word: Focus on CAC and Qualified Leads

Be it B2B or B2C, determining what works best in India depends on your specific industry, ticket size, and audience. The golden rule is to evaluate CAC and the cost of qualified leads before scaling your spending. Qualified leads form the foundation of business growth.

title: Challenges in Lead Generation
description: One of the most common challenges in lead generation is differentiating between prospects who are merely interested and those who have a clear intent to buy.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: One of the most common challenges in lead generation is differentiating between prospects who are merely interested in a product or service and those who have a clear intent to buy. How do you track leads with high intent versus those with high interest? It all boils down to the algorithms and methods used in lead generation, many of which focus on interest but fail to capture intent effectively.

This creates a significant hurdle for businesses, as they need to separate leads based on interest versus intent. While leads generated through Google Ads may have a higher intent, this doesn't entirely solve the problem. Businesses still face the task of identifying and prioritizing high-intent leads while managing costs and resources effectively.
For businesses, the expectation from lead generation agencies or digital marketing teams is simple: deliver high-intent leads with higher conversion rates while using fewer resources.

items: Choosing the Right Lead Generation Platform: Ensuring the platform aligns with the lead persona you're targeting.
Effective Creatives: Designing campaigns that clearly explain the product or service to filter out accidental clicks or casual inquiries.
Streamlined Lead Nurturing: Implementing efficient processes to minimize the time and effort spent chasing unqualified leads.

content.text: If lead generation teams focus on identifying the true lead persona and finding ways to reach out to people with buying intent rather than mere interest, businesses will not only become more profitable but lead generation itself will become more cost-effective from a customer acquisition perspective.

At AOB India, we have mastered the art of generating high-intent qualified leads. Our lead generation methods are data-backed and AI-driven, ensuring that prospects with high intent take priority. This approach has proven to lower customer acquisition costs (CAC) substantially compared to conventional methods.

title: Qualified Lead Generation — The Miyabi Path
description: Qualified Lead generation is often oversimplified as gathering as many contacts as possible. The Miyabi Path focuses on intent-driven lead generation.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: Qualified Lead generation is often oversimplified as an exercise in gathering as many contacts as possible, typically relying on widespread digital channels such as Meta and Google Ads. However, these techniques often result in unqualified, uninterested leads, raising the acquisition cost without meaningful results. In contrast, the Miyabi Path focuses on intent-driven lead generation.

The Miyabi Path involves a sequential process of generating, nurturing, and qualifying leads — a journey that ensures each lead meets strict qualification standards. At its core, this approach leverages advanced AI-driven tools that enable targeted last-mile delivery, capturing leads based on genuine intent rather than passive interest.
1. Kansei Acquisition — Intent-Driven Lead Generation

The initial phase draws on the Japanese concept of Kansei, which combines intuition with sensitivity. This phase is designed to attract leads with true intent, aligning with client needs at a deeper level. AI algorithms masterfully guide this process, blending scientific precision with intuitive selection to go beyond basic data metrics.
2. Shinra Index — Predictive Lead Scoring

The Shinra Index phase uses predictive analytics to score leads based on behavioral signals, engagement patterns, and historical data. This ensures that only the highest-intent leads are passed to the sales team, dramatically improving conversion rates and reducing wasted effort.
3. Data Screening — Disciplined Lead Qualification

Data Screening introduces a rigorous qualification layer where each lead is validated against defined criteria. This disciplined approach eliminates noise from the pipeline and ensures that every prospect that reaches the sales team has been thoroughly vetted.
4. Onkyo Resonance — Harmonic Analysis

The final phase uses harmonic analysis to match leads with the right messaging, timing, and channel. This ensures that communication resonates deeply with the prospect's current state of mind and buying journey.

The Miyabi Path represents an innovative and proven approach to lead generation. Unlike traditional methods, this refined process achieves 15-60% conversion rates, depending on the ticket size and qualification criteria. It offers a revolutionary alternative for businesses seeking quality, intent-driven qualified leads.

title: Top Lead Generation Strategies for Businesses
description: Expert lead generation strategies that increase profitability and operational efficiency for businesses of all sizes.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: How does a bee know which flower has nectar? Honeybees recognize nectar by detecting UV light reflection or by responding to the colour or tone the flower emits to attract pollinators. The same principle applies to lead generation — you need to throw the right light on your business so leads are naturally attracted.
Unlock your true business potential by finding your WHY and HOW. Plan your business flow at three levels: ATTRACT, ENGAGE and CONVERT.
Attract

items: Identify your Target Audience: Understand who your target audience is — luxury segment, middle class, or budget-conscious customers.
Connect with your Audience: Deploy effective advertising, word of mouth marketing, social media, promotions and events, exhibitions and trade shows.

content.text: Engage

items: Generate and Identify potential Leads: Identify the gaps, needs or the problems of your audience for which you are the go-to solution.
Time for trailer: Be hands on with your Demos, FAQs, Case Studies, Comparisons and reviews.

content.text: Convert

items: Time for closure: Give the taste of your product or service to your customer. Don't forget to talk about your pre-sale and post-sale support.
Not the End: Closing a prospect is not the end. One happy client may bring in more references.

content.text: Proven Strategies to Supercharge Business Growth

items: Explore new Markets: Strategic expansion can create a wealth of opportunities.
Build strong relationships with your customers: Transform a regular client to a loyal promoter.
Enhance your social presence: Make sure your content grabs attention and builds curiosity.
Bespoke customer service and Feedback analysis: Customer feedbacks are a channel of learnings.
Upgrade to latest technology and trends: Utilize AI-powered insights to tailor your promotional efforts.
Develop a positive work culture: Investing in your teams' growth is one of the best investments to accelerate your business.

title: Key Challenges in Lead Generation for Indian Startups
description: Start-ups are one of the most interesting topics to discuss in India. A well-designed startup can multiply turnover in a short time and gain global recognition.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: Start-ups are one of the interesting topics to discuss in India. A well-designed and disciplined startup can multiply the turnover in a short time and gain global recognition. India is currently the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, with 1.59 lakh startups.

Despite the fact that the Startup India initiative has been instrumental in creating a thriving entrepreneurial environment nationally, there are some common key challenges in lead generation of startups.
1. Funds and Capital
Adequate capital is the major resource for any start-up. A recent study presents a concerning picture, claiming that 85% of startup businesses are underfunded.
Solution: Start with organic lead generating techniques including social media promotion, content marketing, and referrals. For startups, inbound marketing works very well.
2. Limited Brand Recognition
Larger organizations tend to have a more established brand reputation than startups. Convincing leads to entrust their business to a new or unknown organization may be challenging.
Solution: Prioritize establishing credibility by utilizing influencer relationships, producing relevant quality content, and incorporating evidence of validity.
3. Finding Your Customer

A startup's first strategy is not about setting goals and targets, but to identify potential clients and create tailored lead generation techniques. Your customer is your most valuable business asset.
4. Competitive Market

The two major challenges: people don't know who you are, and why should they trust you? The majority has a tendency to opt for the known brand. Know your competitor, study and analyze their gaps. Never criticize the competition but always aim to outperform them.
5. Team Members

Building an effective team is the vital step in structuring a business plan. One of the successful formulas is the E-Square plan — Expertise and Experience. If startups have to withstand established players, they must come up with innovative strategies integrating the latest technologies.

items: Always treat sales and customer service equally.
Pay equal attention to new clients as well as existing clients.
Always maintain top class service standards.
Effective and cordial communication is the best way to attract your client.
The entrepreneur must be persistent, convincing, and never give up.
To effectively combat competition, possess in-depth domain expertise.

title: Sales Outsourcing: A Game-Changer for Scaling Businesses
description: Sales outsourcing has emerged to fill the gaps in business growth. It is a wise move to leverage the experience of external experts to boost your sales.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: You don't have to be well-versed across each field — like lead generation, sales, and marketing — to launch a business or grow your existing one. If your sales team isn't able to keep up with your business growth, it will quickly become a roadblock. Paying attention to your grey zones is essential.

As sales is one of the major pillars of any business, sales outsourcing has emerged into the market to fill the gaps. It is a wise move to leverage the experience of external experts to boost your sales. The skilled sales representatives are equipped with the latest technologies and tailored strategies to drill down the potential leads, filter and finalize the deal while allowing you to concentrate on managing core business functions.
5 Reasons Why You Need Sales Outsourcing

items: Limited resource and expertise: A key benefit of sales outsourcing is access to well-trained experts equipped with contemporary tools in sales and marketing.
Versatile Needs: Your sales needs might change as per the fluctuating demands of the market. Outsourced sales teams can scale up your business at a faster rate.
Solve capacity issues: Fit-in teams can be outsourced to work on your goals quickly and on a smaller budget.
Focusing on core Business: Free up resources for important business operations.
Cost Efficient: Your marketing, hiring, retraining, management, and retention costs for sales executives will all go down.

content.text: Types of Sales Services That Can Be Outsourced

items: Cold Calling: Establish primary contact and pitch the product or service to identify potential clients.
Building an Elevator Pitch with Lead generation: Earn a second conversation, not just a sale.
Sales Presentation: Data-driven solutions that help navigate customers to the right solutions.
Setting Appointments and Follow ups: Building trust, identifying prospects, qualifying leads, and setting up meetings.
Handling complete sales cycle: All phases from cold calling to closing a deal.

title: In House Sales vs Outsourced Sales: Which One is Right for You
description: Market study says outsourcing sales operations to 3rd parties can achieve higher margins compared to working with in-house teams.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: Market study says outsourcing sales operations to 3rd parties can achieve higher margins compared to working with in-house teams.
Benefits of In-house Sales Team

items: Better control on the team: Broad control on the staffing decisions, setting defined targets and making sure everyone is aligned.
The sales team works only for you: Your company goals will receive more attention, as business growth is equally proportionate to their incentives.
Deep product knowledge: In-house teams undergo company organized trainings and can deal with customer queries more efficiently.
Direct Feedbacks: High-quality customer feedback and quicker solutions to gaps in services.

content.text: Benefits of Outsourced Sales Team

items: Increased profits: Outsourcing sales is more cost-effective — you just pay for the services the team provides.
Explores new markets: Multiple domain knowledge can open gates to hidden markets.
Expertise in specified fields: Well trained on latest technologies and possess cross domain knowledge.

content.text: Though there are remarkable benefits in choosing sales outsourcing from 3rd parties, there are few challenges such as communication barriers, lack of control on team and data protection issues. It is important to be mindful while choosing the right outsourcing company.
Checkpoints for Choosing Sales Outsourcing Services

items: Ensure Industry Experience: A partner with industry-specific knowledge can drastically change the competitive landscape.
Customization and Flexibility: Select a vendor who can adapt to your changing needs.
Setting clear goals: Guidelines that your outsourced team can follow to stay aligned with your company's objectives.
Effective on-boarding and training: Ensure your outsourced sales team fully understands your products, services, and values.
Regular monitoring: Timely feedback ensures the team stays in line with your sales goals.

content.text: Hybrid Approach: Many businesses find success by outsourcing lead generation while keeping in-house teams focused on closing deals.

title: The Role of Sales Outsourcing in B2B Lead Generation
description: Sales outsourcing plays a crucial role in B2B lead generation by optimizing outreach, nurturing prospects, and driving conversions.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: In the boardroom battleground of executive decision-making, one question continues to rise to the surface — Should we outsource sales or keep it in-house? While traditionalists may still view sales outsourcing as a gamble, innovative and growth-focused businesses are increasingly recognizing its strategic value — especially in the realm of B2B lead generation.
Outsourcing is no longer just a cost-cutting measure. It's a scalable, efficient, and ROI-driven approach to sales execution.
1. Risk Mitigation Without Compromise

items: Local intelligence: Outsourced teams bring domain expertise in new geographies — local culture, customer behavior, and regulatory compliance.
No bad hires: You avoid the operational and financial risk of hiring and training sales reps who might not perform.
Built-in compliance: Reputed outsourcing companies understand industry laws and protect you from violations.
Access to proven tech: From CRMs and sales automation tools to analytics dashboards, you benefit from their ecosystem.

content.text: 2. Higher Efficiency, Lower Costs

items: You get an on-demand sales force.
Performance is contract-bound — if results drop, you pivot fast.
You scale up or down without affecting internal morale.
Lead generation is tied to outcomes, not overheads.

content.text: 3. Streamlined Sales Operations

items: Administrative tasks like compliance, reporting, and KPI tracking are offloaded.
Internal conflicts and high attrition issues in sales roles are minimized.
Internal departments see a boost in productivity as operational bandwidth increases.

content.text: 4. Access to Big Data and Global Intelligence

items: Outsourcing partners access and curate AI-driven, behavior-based lead data.
Trained analysts spot buying signals, intent triggers, and profile patterns that an internal team may overlook.
They know how to filter through volumes of data to prioritize high-intent leads with precision.

content.text: 5. Continuous Lead Nurturing and Conversion Optimization

items: Using calls, emails, social media, and more, they keep your brand front-of-mind.
Specialists handle warm leads with patience, empathy, and a structured cadence.
Lead scoring, qualification, and follow-up scheduling is managed with surgical precision.

content.text: Final Thought: Sales Outsourcing Isn't Just Efficient — It's Strategic
In a competitive market, growth depends on speed, accuracy, and consistency. Outsourced sales teams allow businesses to launch faster, target smarter, spend wiser, and convert better.

title: How AI & Automation Are Transforming Sales Outsourcing
description: AI and automation are reshaping sales outsourcing, making it more efficient and data-driven. Learn how businesses leverage AI-powered sales strategies for success.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: In this age, the definition of sales has evolved from the art of persuasion to survival in the AI-governed era. Cold calling, gut instincts, and other manual tasks have been replaced with automation, conversational AI and predictive analytics. Companies that don't respond fast and adapt risk being left behind by competitors who embrace AI and automation tools.
1. The Rise of GTM Engineers

items: GTM Engineers blend creativity with tech skills to serve the entire sales funnel.
Many AI products are not yet market ready — outsourcing companies hire GTM engineers to offer scalable growth.
AOB India leverages such talent to support businesses across 55+ industries.

content.text: 2. Arrival of AI Agents as Teammates

AI Agents are the next chapter of the Artificial Intelligence revolution, making customers more comfortable exploring options. As the agents guide customers through their journey, they take note of needs, preferences, and pain points in the background.

items: AI agents improve customer comfort and reduce friction in communication.
They collect rich contextual data passively during interactions.
AOB India has leveraged such agents to help deliver 3,000+ qualified leads without compromising on personalization.

content.text: 3. Collapse of the Marketing Funnel and the Dawn of Trust Loops

The evolution of the internet has outgrown the marketing funnel into trust loops. Customers create their own non-linear funnel driven by trust. With access to peer feedback, competitive analysis, and curated content before even visiting a site, their decisions are based on emotions, trust, timing and context.
4. Predictive Analytics as the New Navigators

Predictive analytics forecasts sales accurately by considering industry trends, competitors, economic changes, and sales history. All this results in actionable insights with SMART goals for your sales team.
5. Large-Scale Hyper-personalization
Consumers expect highly personalised messaging, but simultaneously want their privacy respected. Finding the sweet spot between personalisation and intrusion has become one of the goals of marketing.
Conclusion: Finding the Right Balance

Success awaits those who strike the right balance between human ingenuity and the efficiency of AI automation. Search for outsourcing partners who use AI agents for data crunching but also understand the value of a human connection for relationship building, creative problem solving, and complex negotiations.

title: 5 Signs Your Business Needs Sales Outsourcing Services
description: Struggling with sales performance? Here are 5 key signs that indicate your business needs sales outsourcing to improve efficiency, cut costs, and drive revenue.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: If you're running a business or managing a sales team, it's easy to fall into the trap of doing it all yourself. Many founders believe that outsourcing any business function means sacrificing control or quality. But the truth is — trying to do everything in-house can often backfire.

When your core team is tangled in administrative tasks or your sales plateau, your growth takes the hit. That's where sales outsourcing steps in — not just as a support system, but as a strategic advantage.
1. Sales Have Plateaued
If your revenue graph looks flat, you may be facing sales plateauing — a stage where momentum stalls despite best efforts.

items: Outsourcing partners bring industry-specific experience.
They offer fresh perspectives, proven playbooks, and data-driven lead generation strategies.
You instantly gain a team ready to diagnose and counter bottlenecks.

content.text: 2. You Need Help Executing New Ideas
Having a great internal strategy team doesn't guarantee conversions. Execution is where ideas live or die.

items: Tap into a diverse talent pool of SDRs, closers, and strategists.
Align execution with your vision using customized sales plans.
Get implementation speed without compromising quality.

content.text: 3. You're Entering a New Market
Breaking into new territory requires a level of precision and speed that's hard to replicate in-house.

items: Training a team from scratch takes months.
Missteps in messaging, positioning, or targeting cost more than money — they cost trust.
Outsourced teams can test and iterate across campaigns quickly.

content.text: 4. Sales Productivity is Declining
A dip in sales conversions is usually a symptom of deeper issues — like lack of training, poor data hygiene, or outdated tools.

items: Outsourced teams come pre-equipped with CRM systems, automation tools, and sales enablement platforms.
They are trained continuously and incentivized to perform.
You eliminate high turnover concerns and recruitment cycles.

content.text: 5. Your Core Team is Stretched Too Thin
If your internal team is handling everything from prospecting to support to content marketing, you're diluting their energy — and your outcomes.

items: Customer experience suffers.
Product innovation slows down.
Brand consistency starts to fade.

content.text: Conclusion: Sales Outsourcing Is a Strategy for Strength

An outsourced sales team provides consistent communication, uniform value articulation, and a replicable system for nurturing leads — using emotionally intelligent conversations, data-backed lead scoring, and personalized engagement.

title: Why Sales Outsourcing is the Future of Scalable Growth
description: Sales outsourcing is revolutionizing business growth by providing scalable, cost-effective solutions. Discover how it helps companies expand faster and stay competitive.
author: Dr. Praveen Kumar

content.text: Sales outsourcing channels the state-of-the-art technology and expertise of outsourcing companies to leverage untapped opportunities and scalable growth. The market has reached a point where focus, scalability and speed have become preset conditions.
1. Personalise the Customer Journey

A brand which doesn't provide personalised experience falls behind in KPIs. Customer acquisition costs drop by 50 percent when the approach is customized, while the marketing ROI increases by 10-30 percent.

items: Sales outsourcing companies can find the sweet spot of personalisation for your company.
AOB India, with experience across 930+ customers, tailors outreach strategies that resonate with unique buyer personas.

content.text: 2. Cut Costs and Focus on Core Competencies

Companies which outsource their sales reported a 79 percent increase in their business growth. Prioritize critical areas of your business such as product development or operations while the outsourced team handles sales.
3. Break Technology Constraints
Investing in the latest tools is not enough. For a business to expand rapidly, experts who can derive the most value out of cutting-edge sales and analytics tools are needed.

items: Using AI, sales professionals analyse countless data points to come up with targeted marketing plans.
AOB India generates 15,000+ raw leads every day, powered by intelligent algorithms and deep analytics.

content.text: 4. Improve Product or Service Rollouts

Businesses can leverage the outsourcing company as a sandbox to test out their new products or services, reaching potential customers to understand the relevance of their offerings before a full launch.
5. Break Silos Within Your Company

items: Outsourcing companies are in a better position to work cross-functionally across departments.
Sales outsourcing professionals introduce objectivity into the company to break data silos.

content.text: 6. Greater Accountability and Performance Management

Outsourcing firms deal with businesses on the basis of their effectiveness in closing deals. They take greater accountability and contribute heavily to increasing revenue, bringing novel ideas and fresh perspectives to the table.

If approached in the right way, outsourcing can become a big competitive advantage. Collaborating with a partner focused on adding new capabilities, with advanced skill sets and expertise in delivering customer satisfaction, is the way to achieve scalable growth.
